example: «تكلم عن» (takallama 'an, to speak about) takes 'an + genitive: تكلم عن مشاكله (he spoke about his problems)
«سأل عن» (sa'ala 'an, to ask about) takes 'an + genitive: سألت عنك (I asked about you). Distinct from سأل + acc (to ask someone)
«تخلى عن» (takhallā 'an, to abandon) takes 'an + genitive: تخلى عن أصدقائه (he abandoned his friends)
«عبر عن» ('abbara 'an, to express) takes 'an + genitive: عبر عن مشاعره (he expressed his feelings)
«اعتذر عن» (i'tadhara 'an, to apologize for) takes 'an + genitive: اعتذر عن خطئه (he apologized for his mistake)
«توقف عن» (tawaqqafa 'an, to stop from) takes 'an + genitive: توقف عن التدخين (he stopped smoking)
«ابتعد عن» (ibta'ada 'an, to move away from) takes 'an + genitive: ابتعد عن الخطر (he moved away from danger)
«دافع عن» (dāfa'a 'an, to defend) takes 'an + genitive: دافع عن وطنه (he defended his homeland)
«أجاب على» (ajāba 'alā, to answer) takes 'alā + genitive: أجاب على السؤال (he answered the question). Also أجاب عن with 'an
«وافق على» (wāfaqa 'alā, to agree to) takes 'alā + genitive: وافق على الاقتراح (he agreed to the proposal)
«اطلع على» (iṭṭala'a 'alā, to look at, get acquainted with) takes 'alā + genitive: اطلع على التقرير (he reviewed the report)
«حافظ على» (ḥāfaẓa 'alā, to preserve, maintain) takes 'alā + genitive: حافظ على صحته (he maintained his health)
«ضحك على» (ḍaḥika 'alā, to laugh at, deceive) takes 'alā + genitive: ضحك علي (he tricked me / he laughed at me)
«عثر على» ('athara 'alā, to find, come across) takes 'alā + genitive: عثرت على المفاتيح (I found the keys)
«حصل على» (ḥaṣala 'alā, to obtain) takes 'alā + genitive: حصل على الجائزة (he obtained the prize)
«حكم على» (ḥakama 'alā, to judge, sentence) takes 'alā + genitive: حكم عليه بالسجن (he sentenced him to prison)
«خاف من» (khāfa min, to fear) takes min + genitive: خاف من الكلب (he was afraid of the dog)
«تعجب من» (ta'ajjaba min, to wonder at) takes min + genitive: تعجب من جرأته (he wondered at his audacity)
«استفاد من» (istafāda min, to benefit from) takes min + genitive: استفدت من الدرس (I benefited from the lesson)
«خرج من» (kharaja min, to exit from) takes min + genitive: خرج من البيت (he left the house)
«اشترى من» (ishtarā min, to buy from) takes min + genitive: اشترى من السوق (he bought from the market)
«غضب من» (ghaḍiba min, to be angry at) takes min + genitive: غضب من الخادم (he was angry at the servant)
«ضحك من» (ḍaḥika min, to laugh at) takes min + genitive: ضحك من النكتة (he laughed at the joke). Compare ضحك على (to laugh AT mockingly)
«اقترب من» (iqtaraba min, to approach) takes min + genitive: اقترب من الباب (he approached the door)
«تعلم من» (ta'allama min, to learn from) takes min + genitive: تعلمت من أبي (I learned from my father)
«شعر بـ» (sha'ara bi-, to feel) takes bi- + genitive: شعر بالخوف (he felt fear)
«اعتنى بـ» ('itanā bi-, to take care of) takes bi- + genitive: تعتني بأطفالها (she takes care of her children)
«رحب بـ» (raḥḥaba bi-, to welcome) takes bi- + genitive: رحب بالضيوف (he welcomed the guests)
«فرح بـ» (fariḥa bi-, to rejoice at) takes bi- + genitive: فرح بنجاحه (he was happy about his success)
«استمتع بـ» (istamta'a bi-, to enjoy) takes bi- + genitive: استمتع بالعطلة (he enjoyed the vacation)
«فاز بـ» (fāza bi-, to win) takes bi- + genitive: فاز بالميدالية (he won the medal)
«اعترف بـ» (i'tarafa bi-, to admit, acknowledge) takes bi- + genitive: اعترف بخطئه (he admitted his mistake)
«اهتم بـ» (ihtamma bi-, to be concerned with) takes bi- + genitive: يهتم بالسياسة (he is interested in politics)
«اتصل بـ» (ittaṣala bi-, to contact) takes bi- + genitive: اتصلت بصديقي (I contacted my friend)
«التقى بـ» (iltaqā bi-, to meet) takes bi- + genitive: التقيت بالمدير (I met the director). Also iltaqā ma'a
«وعد بـ» (wa'ada bi-, to promise) takes bi- + genitive: وعد بالحضور (he promised to come)
«ذكر بـ» (dhakkara bi-, to remind of) takes acc of person + bi- + genitive: ذكرني بالموعد (he reminded me of the appointment)
«بدأ بـ» (bada'a bi-, to begin with) takes bi- + genitive: بدأ بالقراءة (he began with reading)
«أمسك بـ» (amsaka bi-, to grasp, seize) takes bi- + genitive: أمسك بيدي (he held my hand)
«نظر إلى» (naẓara ilā, to look at) takes ilā + genitive: نظرت إليه (I looked at him)
«استمع إلى» (istama'a ilā, to listen to) takes ilā + genitive: استمع إلى الموسيقى (he listened to the music)
«بعث إلى» (ba'atha ilā, to send to) takes ilā + genitive: بعث رسالة إلى أمه (he sent a letter to his mother)
«أشار إلى» (ashāra ilā, to point at, refer to) takes ilā + genitive: أشار إلى الباب (he pointed to the door)
«وصل إلى» (waṣala ilā, to arrive at) takes ilā + genitive: وصل إلى البيت (he arrived home)
«التفت إلى» (iltafata ilā, to turn toward, attend to) takes ilā + genitive: التفت إلى الضيف (he turned to the guest)
«اشتاق إلى» (ishtāqa ilā, to long for) takes ilā + genitive: اشتقت إلى وطني (I longed for my homeland)
«احتاج إلى» (iḥtāja ilā, to need) takes ilā + genitive: أحتاج إلى مساعدة (I need help)
«بحث في» (baḥatha fī, to research, investigate) takes fī + genitive: بحث في القضية (he investigated the case). Distinct from بحث عن (to search for)
«فكر في» (fakkara fī, to think about) takes fī + genitive: فكرت في الموضوع (I thought about the topic)
«رغب في» (raghiba fī, to desire) takes fī + genitive: رغب في الزواج (he desired to marry)
«شارك في» (shāraka fī, to participate in) takes fī + genitive: شارك في المؤتمر (he participated in the conference)
«تأمل في» (ta'ammala fī, to contemplate) takes fī + genitive: تأمل في الحياة (he contemplated life)
«نجح في» (najaḥa fī, to succeed in) takes fī + genitive: نجح في الامتحان (he passed the exam)
«فشل في» (fashila fī, to fail in) takes fī + genitive: فشل في المحاولة (he failed in the attempt). Mirror of najaḥa
«تخصص في» (takhaṣṣaṣa fī, to specialize in) takes fī + genitive: يتخصص في الطب (he specializes in medicine)
«تكلم مع» (takallama ma'a, to talk with) takes ma'a + genitive: تكلم مع المدير (he spoke with the manager). Distinct from takallama 'an (about)
«تحدث مع» (taḥaddatha ma'a, to converse with) takes ma'a + genitive: تحدثت مع جدي (I conversed with my grandfather)
«تعاون مع» (ta'āwana ma'a, to cooperate with) takes ma'a + genitive: تعاون مع زملائه (he cooperated with his colleagues)
«اختلف مع» (ikhtalafa ma'a, to disagree with) takes ma'a + genitive: اختلف مع رأيه (he disagreed with his opinion)
«اتفق مع» (ittafaqa ma'a, to agree with) takes ma'a + genitive: اتفق مع زميله (he agreed with his colleague). Mirror of ikhtalafa ma'a
«كتب لـ» (kataba li-, to write to/for) takes li- + genitive: كتب رسالة لأبيه (he wrote a letter to his father)
«غفر لـ» (ghafara li-, to forgive someone) takes li- + genitive of person: غفر لي (he forgave me). Religious-register frequent
«دعا لـ» (da'ā li-, to pray for, invite) takes li- + genitive: دعا لأخيه بالصحة (he prayed for his brother's health). Compare دعا إلى (to call to)
«أعطى» (a'ṭā, to give) takes double accusative — recipient + thing given: أعطيته كتاباً (I gave him a book)
«علم» ('allama, to teach) takes double accusative: علمته العربية (I taught him Arabic). Form II — causative of 'alima (to know)
«منح» (manaḥa, to grant) takes double accusative: منحه جائزة (he granted him a prize). Formal register
«كسا» (kasā, to clothe) takes double accusative: كساه ثوباً (he clothed him with a garment). Person clothed + garment both in acc
«ظن» (ẓanna, to think, suppose) governs double accusative as a 'verb of the heart': ظنه صديقاً (he thought him a friend). The 'افعال القلوب' (verbs of the heart) take double acc
